title: Fork typelift Algebra → substrate-owned algebraic-typeclass primitives
description: Phase C.3 (PRIORITY 3) of typelift-substrate-modernization. typelift's Algebra and Abstract packages, plus the broomburgo/swift-abstract successor line, form the algebraic-law lineage substrate now needs to bring into 2026. Substrate's `[[substrate-refinement-primitive-vocabulary]]` (NonEmpty, Bounded, NonZero, Trimmed, Slug, AbsolutePath, RelativePath, Sorted, Unique, etc.) traces philosophically to this lineage — refinement types are algebraic invariants over base types. The runtime JSON expectation layer also needs this lineage: issue accumulation behaves like a monoid, severity joins behave like bounded semilattices, and unknown/known/invalid reference states behave like evidence lattices. Fork scope: audit Algebra, Abstract, and swift-abstract API surfaces; identify which typeclasses substrate genuinely needs that aren't subsumed by Swift stdlib; fork or freshly reimplement the missing pieces — likely Semigroup, Monoid, bounded semilattice, lawful evidence lattices, and law-checking helpers. Land at `private/universal/substrate/collectives/wrkstrm-core/private/cross/spm/<slug>/`. Modernization per the TypeLift 2026 fork standard. Possible name: `wrkstrm-algebra` or `swift-algebra-primitives`. Do not copy code from unlicensed sources; use them as design references until license provenance is explicit.
